verificationNote: Telecommunications cloud security requirements constructed from Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, NACSA Act 854, MCMC guidelines, and telecoms cloud security best practices. These are indicative -- verify against current MCMC directives and NACSA sector lead guidance.
applicableStandards: Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588) -- primary telecommunications regulation
CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 (CCM v4)
MCMC General Consumer Code of Practice -- subscriber data handling
Interception of Communications Act 2024 -- lawful interception obligations
MCMC Technical Standards -- network security requirements

subscriberDataProtection.description: Telecommunications subscriber data includes call detail records (CDRs), location data, browsing metadata, subscriber identifiers, and billing information. This data is highly sensitive and subject to strict regulatory controls including lawful interception requirements.
requirements: Subscriber data encrypted at rest and in transit in all cloud services
Strict access controls on CDRs and location data with audit logging
Data residency in Malaysia for subscriber records and CDRs
Lawful interception capability preserved when migrating to cloud architecture
Anonymisation of subscriber data used for analytics and network planning
Retention per MCMC requirements and Interception of Communications Act provisions

keyRequirements.name: 5G core network security in cloud
keyRequirements.description: Cloud-native 5G core network functions (NFs) deployed on public or private cloud must meet telecommunications-grade security requirements. NFV security must address isolation, service availability, and signalling security.
implementation: Dedicated cloud infrastructure for 5G core NFs with strict tenant isolation
Service mesh with mTLS between network functions
Zero-trust architecture for signalling plane (SBI) security
Redundant deployment across multiple availability zones for five-nines availability
Real-time security monitoring of NF health and signalling anomalies

keyRequirements.name: Subscriber data protection in cloud platforms
keyRequirements.description: Call detail records, location data, and subscriber information migrated to cloud must maintain the same security controls as on-premises systems. Lawful interception capability must be preserved.
implementation: Encrypt all subscriber data with customer-managed keys in cloud
Implement data classification separating CDRs, location data, and billing data
Maintain lawful interception interfaces per MCMC technical standards
Access audit trail for all subscriber data queries with 7-year retention
Data residency enforcement in Malaysia for all subscriber data

keyRequirements.name: Network function virtualisation (NFV) security
keyRequirements.description: Virtualised network functions deployed on cloud infrastructure must maintain isolation, performance SLAs, and security monitoring equivalent to physical network elements.
implementation: Container/VM isolation for network functions with dedicated compute resources
Network segmentation between management, signalling, and data planes
Performance monitoring with SLA alerting for latency-sensitive NFs
Automated scaling with security policy inheritance for new instances
Vulnerability management for NFV infrastructure including hypervisor and container runtime

keyRequirements.name: Enterprise cloud service chain security
keyRequirements.description: Telecommunications operators offering enterprise cloud services must manage the security chain from customer to CSP. Shared responsibility must be clearly defined and liability mapped.
implementation: Clear shared responsibility model between operator and customer
Security SLAs in enterprise cloud service agreements
Incident notification chain: CSP to operator to enterprise customer
Regular third-party security assessments of cloud service offering
Data processing agreements covering all parties in the service chain

nacsaObligations.description: NACSA obligations for NCII-designated telecommunications infrastructure using cloud services.
nacsaObligations.applicability: Applies to telecommunications infrastructure designated as NCII under NACSA Act 854. Core network infrastructure, subscriber management systems, and critical communications platforms are likely designated.
obligations: s18: Cloud-hosted telecommunications NCII must have appropriate cybersecurity measures including network function isolation and signalling security
s21: Annual risk assessment covering cloud-hosted telecommunications systems including 5G core and subscriber data platforms
s23: Biennial audit including cloud security posture and NFV security assessment
s26: 6-hour notification for significant cloud security incidents affecting NCII telecommunications systems

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Nation-state targeting of 5G cloud core for intelligence collection
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Subscriber surveillance; national security compromise; signalling manipulation

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: CDR database breach from cloud storage misconfiguration
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Mass subscriber privacy violation; location tracking exposure; regulatory penalty

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: DDoS attack on virtualised network functions causing service outage
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Telecommunications service disruption; emergency services impact; SLA breach

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Supply chain compromise in NFV platform affecting multiple operators
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Multi-operator compromise; national telecommunications infrastructure risk
